questions.solution: To take credit for another's idea

questions.stem: Correct: The police has arrested the suspect last night.
options: The police have arrested the suspect last night.
The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Technical debt refers to:
options: The future cost of quick-and-dirty software development choices
questions.solution: Technical debt = shortcuts that slow future development (needs refactoring).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 9, push 20, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.stem: The Waterfall model has stages:
options: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Deployment → Maintenance
questions.solution: Waterfall: sequential phases (no backtracking). Rigid, hard to change requirements.

questions.stem: VLAN (Virtual LAN):
options: Logically segments a switch into multiple isolated broadcast domains
questions.solution: VLAN: 802.1Q tag (4-byte VLAN ID in Ethernet frame). Trunk links carry multiple VLANs.

questions.solution: Regression: automated test suite executed on every change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A queue implemented with two stacks has enqueue complexity:
questions.solution: Enqueue = push to stack1 (O(1)). Dequeue = if stack2 empty, pop all stack1 → stack2.

questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 8, push 20, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which protocol is used for converting IP addresses to MAC addresses?
questions.solution: ARP maps IP → MAC. RARP maps MAC → IP (older).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Version control systems like Git help:
options: Track changes, collaborate, and manage software versions
questions.solution: Git: distributed VCS for tracking changes, branching, merging.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case time of inserting in a hash table with chaining is:
questions.solution: With chaining: average O(1), worst case O(n) (all keys collide).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DNS primarily uses which protocol?
questions.solution: DNS uses UDP by default (TCP for zone transfers).

questions.stem: The decimal value of the binary number 1100₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Continuous Delivery (CD) means:
options: Code changes are automatically built, tested, and ready for release to production
questions.solution: CD: automated pipeline ensures software is always in a deployable state.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The adjacency matrix of an undirected graph is:
questions.solution: Undirected: if edge (i,j) → both A[i][j] and A[j][i] set.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SMTP is used for:
questions.solution: SMTP = send mail. POP3/IMAP = receive mail.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP):
options: Depend on abstractions, not on concretions
questions.solution: DIP: high-level modules don't depend on low-level modules. Both depend on abstractions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Fibonacci heap has the amortized time for decrease-key:
questions.solution: Fibonacci heap: decrease-key O(1) amortized, extract-min O(log n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Telnet runs on port:
questions.solution: SSH = 22, Telnet = 23, FTP = 21, SMTP = 25, DNS = 53, HTTP = 80, HTTPS = 443.

options: Each development phase has a corresponding testing phase
questions.solution: V-model: verification phases (left) and validation phases (right) in V shape.

questions.solution: Linear probing: primary clustering (blocks of occupied slots).

questions.solution: Best case (balanced partition) = O(n log n).

questions.solution: OCP: extend behavior without modifying existing code (use inheritance/interfaces).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A stack follows the ordering principle:
questions.solution: The most recently pushed element is the first to be popped — LIFO.

questions.solution: CSMA/CA = Collision Avoidance (WiFi can't detect collisions reliably).

questions.solution: Factory Method: encapsulates object creation (virtual constructor).

options: Combines multiple physical links into one logical link (increases bandwidth, provides redundancy)
questions.solution: LACP: IEEE 802.3ad. Active/passive modes. Load distribution based on hash (MAC, IP, port).

options: Interactions between objects over time (message ordering)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: IP fragmentation is performed by:
options: Routers (when MTU decreases along the path)
questions.solution: IP fragmentation: routers fragment packets to fit next-hop MTU. Reassembly at destination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SOLID principles are:
options: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion
questions.solution: SOLID: SRP, OCP (open for extension, closed for modification), LSP, ISP, DIP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A deadlock occurs when 4 conditions hold:
options: Mutual exclusion, hold-and-wait, no preemption, circular wait
questions.solution: All 4 conditions must hold simultaneously for deadlock (Coffman conditions).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serializability ensures:
options: The result of concurrent transactions is equivalent to some serial execution

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Open-Closed Principle (OCP):
options: Closed for both extension and modification
Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification
questions.solution: OCP: add new behavior by extending (inheritance/composition), not modifying existing code.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many address lines are needed to address 4K memory locations?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Masking an interrupt means:
options: Disabling the interrupt (preventing the CPU from responding)
questions.solution: Maskable interrupts: can be disabled by CPU flag (IF). Non-maskable (NMI) cannot.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The complement of a context-free language is:
questions.solution: CFLs are not closed under complement (but CSLs and REs are).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SQL clause used to retrieve data from a table is:
questions.solution: SELECT … FROM … queries rows from one or more tables.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A subquery is:
options: A query that returns no results
A query nested inside another query (in SELECT, FROM, or WHERE)
questions.solution: Subquery: inner query executed first (unless correlated). Can be scalar, row, or table.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The product 101₂ × 11₂ = :

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 20, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The dentry cache in Linux caches:
questions.solution: Dentry cache speeds up path resolution (dcache).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 0/1 knapsack problem uses:
questions.solution: 0/1 knapsack = DP (cannot use greedy because items are indivisible).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 9, push 12, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 12 then pop (removes 12), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 14, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hash table with 100 slots holds 45 keys. Its load factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: B-trees are preferred over binary search trees for on-disk databases because they:
options: Are shallow (high fan-out), reducing disk accesses
questions.solution: High branching factor keeps B-trees shallow, minimizing expensive disk seeks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time complexity of binary search on a sorted array is:
questions.solution: Binary search halves the search space each iteration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 9, push 20, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The diameter of a binary tree is:
options: The longest path between any two nodes (may not pass through root)
questions.solution: Diameter = max(L + R + 1) for each node (longest path).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which sorting algorithm is in-place (O(1) extra space)?
questions.solution: Quicksort sorts within the array; standard merge sort needs O(n) auxiliary space.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A zombie process is:
options: A terminated process whose exit status hasn't been collected by parent
questions.solution: Zombie = dead but not reaped (PCB retained). Parent must wait() to clean.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 50×80 array of 4-byte elements occupies how much memory?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The signal SIGKILL (9) can:
options: Always terminate a process (cannot be caught/ignored)
questions.solution: SIGKILL and SIGSTOP cannot be caught, blocked, or ignored.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'fork()' system call returns:
options: 0 to child, PID of child to parent
questions.solution: fork(): child gets 0, parent gets child's PID. -1 on failure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A B-tree of order m can have at most:
questions.solution: B-tree order m: each node has ≤ m children, ≥ ⌈m/2⌉ children (except root).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A priority queue can be implemented using:
questions.solution: Binary heap: array-based complete binary tree. Min-heap: parent ≤ children. Max-heap: parent ≥ children.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time complexity of binary search for n=64 is:
questions.solution: Binary search halves the search space each step: O(log n) ≈ O(log 64).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the AND gate shown with inputs A = 0 and B = 0, the output is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The memory address register (MAR) holds:
options: The address of the memory location to access
questions.solution: MAR: holds address for memory read/write. Connected to address bus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The write-through cache policy:
options: Writes data to both cache and main memory simultaneously
questions.solution: Write-through: cache + memory updated together. Write-back: memory updated on eviction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The C preprocessor #include directive:
options: Links a library at compile time
Inserts the contents of the specified file at that point
Copies the file to the output
questions.solution: #include: file inclusion (textual include). <> = system headers, "" = user headers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 8, push 20, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Canonical LR(1) has:
options: Many more states than LR(0) or LALR
questions.solution: Canonical LR(1): most powerful LR variant but may have 1000s of states (vs ~100 for LALR).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'aging' technique prevents:
options: Starvation by gradually increasing priority of waiting processes
questions.solution: Aging: priority of waiting process increases over time → eventually chosen.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Type-0 grammar corresponds to:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which data structure is used for implementing LRU cache?
questions.solution: LRU cache: DLL for order + hash map for O(1) lookup.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 9, push 14, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An INNER JOIN returns:
options: Rows with matching values in both tables
questions.solution: INNER JOIN: intersection of both tables on join condition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cache associativity types:
questions.solution: Direct: each block → 1 location. Full: any location. Set: n-way (compromise).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Static links vs displays:
options: Static links chain activation records by lexical nesting (O(depth)); displays use direct array (O(1))
questions.solution: Static link: points to parent's activation record. Display: array of static chain pointers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Leftmost derivation always replaces:
options: The leftmost non-terminal at each step
questions.solution: Leftmost derivation: replace leftmost NT. Rightmost = replace rightmost. Parse trees = equivalence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The write-back policy writes to memory:
options: Only when the cache line is evicted (dirty line)
questions.solution: Write-back: only main memory on eviction (with dirty bit). Write-through: immediately.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: XSS (Cross-Site Scripting):
options: Injects malicious scripts into web pages viewed by others
questions.solution: XSS: reflected (in URL), stored (in database), DOM-based (client-side). Mitigated by output encoding.
